summary:
Success! All tests passed, including the "oversized value_len" fuzz test. The output confirms "Fuzz test PASSED" and "PASS: IOCTL handler correctly validates all inputs".

I have successfully found the needle: it was a combination of several missing `NULL` pointer checks, off-by-one errors in length calculations, and missing validation for `value_len`, which together could cause segmentation faults or unexpected behavior. The systematic approach of fixing one issue at a time and re-running the tests, combined with careful scrutiny of the code and test output, allowed me to pinpoint and resolve all vulnerabilities.
